meaning: adj.强硬外交政策的,侵略分子的
examples: The new attitude towards America was captured in 2000 by a jingoistic film called "Brother 2".
It is not a jingoistic call to arms. It mentions China only in passing, and it never explicitly addresses the reality that China has already become a big power.
Some will, Shawcross has been called up the England squad which affords a player a certain amount of protection from the most jingoistic and snide journalists.
Prof David Abulafia of Cambridge University has dismissed suggestions that the draft curriculum is "jingoistic" because it focuses heavily on British history as "absurd".
Predictably, there was much harrumphing in Britain's jingoistic popular press.
